name: Dagger
rulesSummary: The first dagger carried by each warrior is free; additional daggers cost 2 gc.

name: Mace
rulesSummary: Common one-handed close combat weapon.

name: Hammer
rulesSummary: Common one-handed close combat weapon.

name: Club
rulesSummary: Common one-handed close combat weapon from the Skaven henchmen equipment list.

name: Sigmarite Warhammer
rulesSummary: Sisters-only holy warhammer. Strength as user +1, with Concussion and Holy Weapon special rules.
restrictions: Common for Sisters of Sigmar only. Only Matriarchs and Sister Superiors may carry two.

name: Axe
rulesSummary: Common one-handed close combat weapon.

name: Sword
rulesSummary: Common one-handed close combat weapon.

name: Double-handed Weapon
rulesSummary: Two-handed close combat weapon available from several warband lists.

name: Spear
rulesSummary: Close combat weapon available to Zealots in the Witch Hunter list.

name: Halberd
rulesSummary: Close combat weapon available from the Mercenary equipment list.

name: Weeping Blades
rulesSummary: Skaven-only paired swords that are permanently venomous and can parry.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only.

name: Fighting Claws
rulesSummary: Skaven-only paired claws that grant an extra attack, help climbing, allow parries and cannot be combined with other weapons.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only. Cumbersome: may not be used with other weapons.

name: Disease Dagger
rarity: Rare 8, Skaven of Clan Pestilens only
rulesSummary: Clan Pestilens dagger. It gives the enemy the normal dagger armour-save bonus and can inflict an extra wound on a natural 6 to hit if the target fails a Toughness test.
restrictions: Skaven of Clan Pestilens heroes equipment list only.

name: Censer
rarity: Rare 8, Skaven of Clan Pestilens only
rulesSummary: Clan Pestilens two-handed flail. It has +2 Strength in the first round of each combat, Concussion, and plague fumes that can wound models in base contact after attacks are resolved.
restrictions: Skaven of Clan Pestilens heroes equipment list only.

name: Flail
rulesSummary: Flagellant close combat weapon.

name: Flail
rulesSummary: Brute equipment list flail at the Carnival of Chaos listed recruitment cost.
restrictions: Carnival of Chaos Brutes only through the Brute equipment list.

name: Steel Whip
rulesSummary: Sisters-only whip. Cannot be parried and has the Whipcrack special rule.
restrictions: Common for Sisters of Sigmar only.

name: Morning Star
rulesSummary: Flagellant close combat weapon.

name: Bow
rulesSummary: Common missile weapon available to Zealots in the Witch Hunter list.

name: Bow
rulesSummary: Bow bought from the Carnival of Chaos equipment list at its listed recruitment cost.
restrictions: Carnival of Chaos equipment list only.

name: Sling
rulesSummary: Simple missile weapon available to Sisters of Sigmar.

name: Throwing Stars
rulesSummary: Skaven hero missile weapon from the Clan Eshin equipment list.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only.

name: Blowpipe
rulesSummary: Short-ranged Skaven-only poisoned missile weapon that can fire while hidden without automatically revealing the shooter.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only.

name: Short Bow
rulesSummary: Common missile weapon available to Zealots in the Witch Hunter list.

name: Short Bow
rulesSummary: Short bow bought from the Carnival of Chaos equipment list at its listed recruitment cost.
restrictions: Carnival of Chaos equipment list only.

name: Crossbow
rulesSummary: Missile weapon available to Witch Hunter heroes.

name: Pistol
rulesSummary: Blackpowder pistol bought singly.

name: Brace of Pistols
rulesSummary: A pair of pistols. For warband creation weapon limits, a brace counts as one missile weapon.

name: Warplock Pistol
rulesSummary: Skaven-only pistol with high Strength and a fire-every-other-turn restriction.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only.

name: Brace of Warplock Pistols
rulesSummary: A pair of warplock pistols. For this app's warband creation weapon limits, a brace counts as one missile weapon.
restrictions: Skaven heroes equipment list only.

name: Crossbow Pistol
rulesSummary: Special pistol-style missile weapon available to Witch Hunter heroes.

name: Duelling Pistol
rulesSummary: Pistol-style missile weapon available from the Mercenary equipment list.

name: Brace of Duelling Pistols
rulesSummary: A pair of duelling pistols. For this app's warband creation weapon limits, a brace counts as one missile weapon.

name: Long Bow
rulesSummary: Missile weapon available to Mercenary Marksmen.

name: Blunderbuss
rulesSummary: Blackpowder missile weapon available to Mercenary Marksmen.

name: Handgun
rulesSummary: Blackpowder missile weapon available to Mercenary Marksmen.

name: Hunting Rifle
rulesSummary: Expensive long-ranged missile weapon available to Mercenary Marksmen.

name: Light Armour
rulesSummary: Body armour option from the relevant equipment lists.

name: Heavy Armour
rulesSummary: Body armour option for Witch Hunter heroes.

name: Shield
rulesSummary: Armour option from the relevant equipment lists.

name: Buckler
rulesSummary: Armour option for Witch Hunter heroes.

name: Helmet
rulesSummary: Armour option from the relevant equipment lists.

name: Holy Tome
rulesSummary: Heroine-only miscellaneous equipment from the Sisters list.
restrictions: Heroines only.

name: Blessed Water
rulesSummary: Heroine-only miscellaneous equipment from the Sisters list.
restrictions: Heroines only.

name: Holy Relic
rulesSummary: Heroine-only miscellaneous equipment from the Sisters list.
restrictions: Heroines only.

name: Stream of Corruption
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. Gives the Tainted One a short-ranged Strength 3 shooting attack that allows no armour save.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Nurgle's Rot
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One is immune to poison and can pass Nurgle's Rot to living enemies in close combat.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Cloud of Flies
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. Enemies fighting the Tainted One suffer a to-hit penalty in close combat.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Bloated Foulness
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. Gives +1 Wound and +1 Toughness, but reduces Movement by 1.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Mark of Nurgle
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. Gives +1 Wound and immunity to poison.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Hideous
rulesSummary: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One causes Fear.
restrictions: Tainted Ones only, bought at recruitment. Second and later Blessings cost double.

name: Squig Prodder
rulesSummary: Goblin-only spear-like weapon. Cave Squigs respect a prodder within the larger listed Minderz range.
restrictions: Common, Goblins only.

name: Mad Cap Mushrooms
rulesSummary: Goblin drug required to use a Ball and Chain; a Goblin may take the mushrooms at the start of any turn.
restrictions: Common for Orc warbands that include Goblins.

name: Ball and Chain
rulesSummary: Goblin-only heavy weapon used with Mad Cap Mushrooms. It strikes at user Strength +2, ignores armour saves and can cause multiple wounds, but moves randomly and is dangerous to the wielder.
restrictions: Common, Goblins only. Requires Mad Cap Mushrooms.

name: Elf Bow
rulesSummary: Range 36 inches, Strength 3 missile weapon with a -1 armour save modifier.
restrictions: Available from the Shadow Warrior equipment list; rare outside creation.

name: Ithilmar Dagger
rulesSummary: Ithilmar version of a dagger at the Shadow Warrior creation cost; grants +1 Initiative in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation; app models the listed 2x weapon price as a separate item.

name: Ithilmar Sword
rulesSummary: Ithilmar version of a sword at the Shadow Warrior creation cost; grants +1 Initiative in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation; app models the listed 2x weapon price as a separate item.

name: Ithilmar Spear
rulesSummary: Ithilmar version of a spear at the Shadow Warrior creation cost; grants +1 Initiative in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation; app models the listed 2x weapon price as a separate item.

name: Ithilmar Double-handed Weapon
rulesSummary: Ithilmar version of a double-handed weapon at the Shadow Warrior creation cost; grants +1 Initiative in hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation; app models the listed 2x weapon price as a separate item.

name: Ithilmar Armour
rulesSummary: 5+ armour save. It does not reduce Movement when combined with a shield.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation.

name: Standard of Nagarythe
rulesSummary: Second rally point for Shadow Warriors within 12 inches; if captured, the warband gains Hatred for the rest of the game and may not voluntarily rout.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only; may only be bought when the warband is created.

name: War Horn of Nagarythe
rulesSummary: Once per game at the start of a Shadow Warrior turn, all members gain +1 Leadership, up to 10, until the start of the next turn.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation.

name: Elven Cloak
rulesSummary: Enemy shooting attacks suffer an additional -1 to hit the wearer.
restrictions: Available from the Shadow Warrior equipment list.

name: Elven Wine
rulesSummary: One-use item. A Shadow Warrior warband that drinks it before a battle is immune to Fear for that battle.
restrictions: Shadow Warrior heroes only at creation; one use only.

name: Elven Runestones
rulesSummary: A mage may attempt to dispel a spell cast against himself or another warband member by matching the casting score.
restrictions: Shadow Weavers only at creation.

name: Stone Axe
rulesSummary: Lizardmen stone axe that counts as a club.
restrictions: Lizardmen equipment lists only.

name: Light Armour
rulesSummary: Lizardmen light armour at the warband's listed 50 gc cost; adds +1 to Scaly Skin saves.
restrictions: Lizardmen armour is rare and always costs 50 gc.

name: Bone Helmet
rulesSummary: Lizardmen helmet; Skink Priests and Saurus may use it from their equipment lists.
restrictions: Saurus equipment list, and Skink Priest only from the Skinks list.

name: Bolas
rulesSummary: Range 16 inch one-use missile weapon. It entangles rather than wounds and is recovered after the battle.
restrictions: Common, Lizardmen only.

name: Javelin
rulesSummary: Range 8 inch thrown spear using the thrower's Strength; no range or moving penalties.
restrictions: Common missile weapon for Skinks and similar hunters.

name: Throwing Knives
rulesSummary: Skink missile weapon from the Lizardmen equipment list.
restrictions: Skinks equipment list only.

name: Blowpipe
rarity: Rare 7, Lizardmen and Forest Goblins only
rulesSummary: Blowpipe from the Lizardmen equipment list; use the core blowpipe weapon rules.
restrictions: Skinks equipment list only.

name: Dark Venom
rulesSummary: Common Lizardmen poison purchase for Skink heroes; may only be used on missile weapons.
restrictions: Skink heroes only; missile weapons only.

name: Black Lotus
rulesSummary: Common Lizardmen poison purchase for Skink heroes; may only be used on missile weapons.
restrictions: Skink heroes only; missile weapons only.

name: Reptile Venom
rulesSummary: One-battle Skink henchman poison for missile weapons. Adds +1 Strength but no additional save modifier.
restrictions: Skink henchmen missile weapons only; henchman groups must be armed uniformly.

name: Oversized Jaws
rulesSummary: Sacred Marking. The Saurus hero's bite attack gains +1 Strength.
restrictions: Saurus heroes only; only one Sacred Marking per hero and bought at recruitment.

name: Poison Glands
rulesSummary: Sacred Marking. The Skink hero may make bite attacks instead of weapon attacks; those bites improve injury rolls.
restrictions: Skink heroes only; only one Sacred Marking per hero and bought at recruitment.

name: Mark of the Old Ones
rulesSummary: Sacred Marking. Once per battle, the hero may change one failed roll into a success for an action he is making himself.
restrictions: Lizardmen heroes only; only one Sacred Marking per hero and bought at recruitment.

name: Kroxigor Halberd
rulesSummary: The Kroxigor is equipped with a halberd; its cost is included in the Kroxigor hire cost.
restrictions: Kroxigor only.

name: Spear
rulesSummary: Forest Goblin spear bought at the warband's listed cost. It uses spear rules including striking first in the first round of hand-to-hand combat.
restrictions: Forest Goblin equipment lists only.

name: Boss Pole
rulesSummary: Forest Goblin hero badge of office. It acts as a spear and lets the hero and Goblin henchmen within 6 inches ignore Animosity.
restrictions: Common, Forest Goblin heroes only.

name: Blowpipe
rulesSummary: Range 8 inch Strength 1 missile weapon with Poison and Stealthy. It improves the target's armour save by +1 and cannot cause critical hits.
restrictions: Common for Forest Goblins.

name: Throwing Weapons
rulesSummary: Forest Goblin thrown weapons. Sluggas may throw these up to three times in the shooting phase.
restrictions: Forest Goblin henchmen equipment list.

name: Poisoned Weapon
rulesSummary: Forest Goblin poison purchase applied to one weapon. That weapon adds +1 to injury rolls and may not be traded or sold later.
restrictions: Common, Forest Goblins only. The app records this as one poisoned weapon note; choose the affected weapon in notes.

name: Magic Gubbinz
rulesSummary: Shaman focus items. The Shaman may re-roll a failed magic test on a D6 roll of 4+.
restrictions: Forest Goblin Shaman only.

name: Red Toof Tribal Jewellery
rulesSummary: Permanent body-piercing trophy that makes the model subject to Frenzy.
restrictions: Forest Goblins only; cannot be removed once bought.

name: Riding/Draft Horse
rulesSummary: Optional mounted rules. Human Hero mount with profile M8 WS1 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I3 A0 Ld5. It gives the standard mounted armour-save bonus but does not attack in close combat.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Human Heroes only.

name: Warhorse
rulesSummary: Optional mounted rules. Human Hero combat mount with profile M8 WS3 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I3 A1 Ld5. Battle Schooled lets the rider re-roll one failed loss of control test.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Human Heroes only.

name: Barding
rulesSummary: Optional mounted rules. Warhorse armour adds a further +1 armour save bonus to the mounted model, reduces the mount's Movement by 1, and makes the warhorse die only on a serious injury roll of 1 if the rider goes out of action.
restrictions: Warhorse only.

name: Nightmare
rulesSummary: Optional Blazing Saddles mount. Undead mount with profile M8 WS2 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I2 A1 Ld5. It may not run, is immune to poison and psychology, and uses the mounted warrior rules.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Vampires and Necromancers only.

name: War Boar
rulesSummary: Optional Blazing Saddles mount. Orc mount with profile M7 WS3 BS0 S3 T4 W1 I3 A1 Ld3. Its own attack gains +2 Strength on the charge and it gives the rider an additional +1 armour save bonus.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Orc Heroes only.

name: Cold One
rulesSummary: Optional Blazing Saddles mount. Lizardmen mount with profile M7 WS3 BS0 S4 T4 W1 I3 A1 Ld3. It causes Fear, is subject to Stupidity, and gives the rider an additional +1 armour save bonus.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Lizardmen Heroes only.

name: Elven Steed
rulesSummary: Optional Blazing Saddles mount. Elf mount with profile M9 WS3 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I4 A1 Ld5. Battle Schooled lets the rider re-roll one failed loss of control test.
restrictions: Optional mounted combat rules. Shadow Warrior Heroes only in the current seeded warbands.

name: Giant Spider
rulesSummary: Forest Goblin spider mount. Profile M7 WS3 BS0 S3(4) T3 W1 I4 A1 Ld4 with poisoned attacks and Wall Walk.
restrictions: Goblin spider mount; full mounted-rider handling remains manual.
